feed.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage

entry.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
entry.summary: The email touches on the process of sharing and discussing a Bitcoin Improvement Proposal (BIP). The sender suggests that after meeting the criteria for presenting and discussing an idea, the next step might involve opening a Pull Request (PR) in the BIPs repository. Additionally, there's a proposal to share the draft on a mailing list, citing the different yet overlapping audience that could provide varied insights or feedback. The sender expresses some frustration about not being able to convey their point effectively but plans to attempt again on the repository, hoping that either they or someone else can articulate it more clearly. This approach indicates a strategy to gather as much constructive feedback as possible by engaging with both platforms, which could lead to a more refined and effective proposal.
